sentences.text: So what--
Now I can tell you.
When I came near [INAUDIBLE] told me that she didn't bake the bread.
And she don't have to-- what to give me because they was all-- all day looking for the Jews, the Germans.
And she's afraid to stay with me to talk when somebody catch him.
They will kill her.
So I started crying.
Ready?
Ready?
So I start crying [INAUDIBLE] I came then [INAUDIBLE] wore that day.
The children slips out-- out of blue.
I can't-- so the [INAUDIBLE] told me, you not go [INAUDIBLE] give the-- the-- the flour.
And she will bake [? along ?] the bread.
So they did it.
The two of them come up from Auschwitz afraid to be with me.
And it was true.
Because I didn't-- they didn't tell me.
But I-- later, I know that it was all the Nazis all there.
But they was looking.
If she would be with me in the [INAUDIBLE], they would kill you right away.
[INAUDIBLE] So-- so her husband told me meet me at the [INAUDIBLE] in-- in the [? 2 ?] [? o'clock. ?] We'll go to the [NON-ENGLISH], you know, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].
And to make the bread, prepare the bread.
And I start preparing [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].
Yeah.
[NON-ENGLISH].
And I was so [INAUDIBLE].
She came over to our forest to kill us.
I-- I didn't-- didn't want to believe it.
So-- but when I gave the bread in [INAUDIBLE].
So I opened the door.
And I came up from there to see [INAUDIBLE].
You-- you understand me?
Yeah.
Oh, so I heard that this is from the site from the forest where my children-- all-- all our Yiddish children are so many, 87, all of them children.
Their mothers and fathers, they are not here.
And-- and I am here.
I'm the only-- I'm the only mother for them.
So when I was standing-- so he came up, her husband.
Go in.
If Nazi will kill you and [INAUDIBLE] this-- this And I tell him I hear something is in the forest now.
No, no, dear.
You think so.
This is somebody else.
They want to scare you.
They want to scare you.
But I came in.
And I take up in the oven.
Then I came in the forest [INAUDIBLE] beginning-- beginning of the day.
My god, my god-- what I saw, all of them, all of the children are-- they took a tarp.
And all of them lay in the forest.
Oh my god, and I came to-- to [INAUDIBLE].
So my-- my children [INAUDIBLE].
The-- the little girl, I gave her-- I got her a little shawl.
I gave her when I left.
I pulled it [? in here. ?]
So the shawl was here.
And she got blonde curls, so beautiful-- like asleep.
So she got up [INAUDIBLE].
It was asleep in-- in the shoulder.
And all around, we bled.
And my boy, something in here on the feet.
And I came.
And I took [INAUDIBLE] was a word.
And still I came.
And the two of them was like they are sleep.
And then near them, there were two children also full and blue.
I didn't-- I didn't look up there.
If I look, I didn't see there nothing [INAUDIBLE].
And so was-- it was with us, my-- my sisters, one boy-- one boy, yeah, I forgot before to tell you a story from the work, when I'll work.
So I worked here since before I came and before [INAUDIBLE].
So one was with-- with me in the grave [INAUDIBLE].
He was-- they gave him not a pull, but they was all blue.
They gave him something with something.
And so was the latest husband also-- something also.
The children [INAUDIBLE] with pulls.
But because it was blood around, the [INAUDIBLE] wasn't the [INAUDIBLE] blood.
But [INAUDIBLE] delighted [INAUDIBLE] to children, I didn't find it.
So I was going-- I-- I-- I took up my two children to the grave.
I took up the-- the little-- little boy, little-- little children also.
I put them.
And I was with them four days.
And I was so bawling.
I was like a little [INAUDIBLE] pool.
And I will be with them.
I have a good place.
I will be with them.
But nobody came.
In-- in-- in [? 19 ?] year, nothing.
For the fifth day, I went out.
And I was thinking where are the ladies?
They are the children's mother.
So I was looking around.
And I see over there like-- like a little [NON-ENGLISH], you know what I mean?
[INAUDIBLE] I go with this and I saw him standing [INAUDIBLE] this.
Otherwise, I wouldn't be alive.
I went crying in the grave and I [INAUDIBLE].
The-- the-- I break the bread, what I-- what I got [INAUDIBLE].
When I came and see the children, so I took everything-- I threw [? at that. ?] I wouldn't eat.
The children come here.
I will need [INAUDIBLE].
I-- I threw out.
So I didn't have nothing in the [INAUDIBLE].
So the-- so I was leaving without bread, without water, and with nothing only to-- and with my tears.
And it was already the fifth day.
And I was alive.
And I could walk.
I-- I came and I found it-- I see somebody over there.
She was all naked.
Breast was cut in pieces.
She was such a beautiful lady with colored black on the hair.
And-- and the [INAUDIBLE] in the [INAUDIBLE] place of mothers, you know the place, they put out such a big-- stay and look for [NON-ENGLISH].
So I decided-- I decided I will try to go on with life.
Because they will catch me.
[INAUDIBLE] They will do the same thing not to [INAUDIBLE] on their end.
This make me-- so I decided.
I was standing, crying [NON-ENGLISH].
And I decided I think go.
I wouldn't let my body [INAUDIBLE] on their end.
You see, I was there in the-- in the deep [INAUDIBLE].
[PAUSES FOR 6 SECONDS] And what shall I do?
I came back to the-- to the-- and I-- my-- and I look.
And I didn't know I was [INAUDIBLE] or to stay here or go out and go up.
And then I was looking [INAUDIBLE] pieces of paper, our piece of paper.
Over there, I start writing in the grave here.
We got [INAUDIBLE] with grass from the [INAUDIBLE] used to cover us.
Nobody was [INAUDIBLE].
But sometime, was-- was a little raining or was-- we-- we put it out and [? no one ?] was writing.
So this-- first of all, so the nanny, she gave my boy papers and pencils and crayons because he was [? over ?] to-- for six-- when he was six years old, he was [? over ?] in a big town in a school.
And I got to-- letters from the-- from the teachers.
I-- your child will be somebody.
It was whatever he saw.
He was a [NON-ENGLISH], a horse, a man-- a-- he was-- he was so good.
He was over eight years old.
So he was saving marks.
And then she told us that she don't want all of it, and that she gave us a [INAUDIBLE] [NON-ENGLISH]-- clothing.
Clothing, yeah.
So she gave me.
She gave me pencils and-- and crayons and because she know that he likes to [INAUDIBLE] took what I was writing.
Because one day, I-- I-- we were sitting and I was crying.
So he told me-- he told me, Mama, why you crying?
Take a pencil.
Write.
[INAUDIBLE] He gave me a piece of paper.
And he gave me a pencil.
Write something.
So the-- the first words-- maybe if you will find somewhere, I-- I believe I gave this [INAUDIBLE].
It's-- it's a nice [INAUDIBLE] write.
What shall I write?
I write it was a mother and two children.
And I do whatever I can to-- to help the two children.
But I saw every day is worse and worse.
That's what do we do here?
So I tell him, maybe some Jew will come here, will find our papers here.
So we'll find that your mother and [INAUDIBLE] few was with me.
This was the first what I start writing [? over the orphan. ?] From this [INAUDIBLE] many, many times.
But the rest is already written when I was [INAUDIBLE].
But I was still [INAUDIBLE] Hitler's [INAUDIBLE] today here and today there and--
This-- this was in '43.
In '43 was the-- the children killed.
So I-- I-- I left the forest there.
Then I decided to go on.
So I-- I left the forest.
Oh, in [INAUDIBLE] in straw in hay, straw.
There was straw, I came in.
In some city, they [INAUDIBLE].
But I got a few [INAUDIBLE] was working for us in the [INAUDIBLE].
So
So I come and see.
I know they wouldn't killed me.
So I was going in the night for a-- for a piece of bread and a drink.
And-- and-- and the rest, I was in the straw and hay in some-- in some graves that I find.
And so-- and so till then.
So you were just hiding?
Hiding.
[INAUDIBLE] I was hiding.
I was in a-- I don't know if you know so much straw, a [NON-ENGLISH].
So I was in the-- in the-- Then I-- I yield.
And the Russian came.
I still was hiding.
When the Russian came, and-- there were singing in-- in Russian.
And I understand Russian.
So till-- till the end of the war, I was-- but in [INAUDIBLE], I was the lady.
Her husband was also working with us.
And-- and I didn't-- I didn't have the children [INAUDIBLE] was alone.
So I came in the night, and I was waiting.
I saw her go out.
So she came out.
And she thought [INAUDIBLE] she, when I got my children, I come to the town to my [NON-ENGLISH], to my mother over there in hospital.
So she brought me diapers for the children to the hospital then you see was the-- I-- anyway.
So I didn't [INAUDIBLE] your-- your husband or your son.
I was afraid.
So she thought-- she took me to the-- to the store.
These are the [? lists. ?]
She took me to the store.
And she [INAUDIBLE] day, she brought me something [INAUDIBLE] milk and pretzel and she brought-- she brought me something.
The end [INAUDIBLE] when-- when I [INAUDIBLE] the Russian.
So she came and I hear.
Then she want to call me, that she is-- so she called me.
She-- they got a-- a dog.
So she called the dog's name.
Yeah.
I-- I will call the dog's name and you will know I am here.
But this time, [INAUDIBLE] in the Russia.
So I was in the [INAUDIBLE] to go out, very near.
So I heard she's talking with somebody.
So I was so afraid.
Maybe she gave me now.
I even hear-- hear neighbor [INAUDIBLE].
What can I do?
So I-- I hear Sonia, come up.
Come-- come up.
This is my husband and don't be afraid for him.
He wouldn't do you nothing.
So I came up, and he took me off his back.
And they took me along.
And they make [INAUDIBLE] bed.
And they hold me a few days.
In the few days, I just-- what I will do with goyim.
I was thinking I am the only Jew.
Really, over [? there is-- ?] But not too many Jews.
What-- so I started crying for them.
So even-- even town-- in-- in another little town.
And over there, you find [? over there ?] three Jews-- was up from there.
So they took me over there and [INAUDIBLE].
That was--
This was not all.
Over there is more.
Because I was alone.
I was [INAUDIBLE]-- it was [INAUDIBLE] it's more [INAUDIBLE].
Oh, yeah, with these Jews, I stayed in-- this was over there.
Well, we-- we was already 13 or 12-- two, three men and the rest women.
So we was over there.
And we saw what we have to eat.
We have-- we don't have a dress, and we don't have nothing.
So I was going.
But the lady gave it to me.
She works [INAUDIBLE] blouse with the-- so I mostly was Polish.
Like, they don't understand Russian.
I understand Russian.
So I decided [INAUDIBLE] was sitting also in-- over there was all the little owls.
Whenever I came, I was thinking the owls will fall-- fall fast.
But we was over there on some straw and all of the [INAUDIBLE].
So I told them we will-- we will go-- again, they-- we will go to the Russians.
And the Russians, we not-- we're not afraid.
But there, Stalin was still [INAUDIBLE].
So the-- the whole over there, the old boss was a [INAUDIBLE], was a Jew.
So we took-- for tomorrow, we took through-- three, me and two more.
And we was going.
And told them the-- the story.
So he gave us work in a-- in the kitchen.
You see, the kitchen came in from the-- from the-- from the war.
But the soldiers are-- full of soldiers over there [INAUDIBLE].
So he gave us in the kitchen.
In the kitchen, we was all right.
We worked in the kitchen, and we eat in the kitchen.
And they made us-- he took-- he came and saw how we sleep.
I told him our little story.
He shall tell me [INAUDIBLE] to see what is here.
And he came in, and he gave us places to sleep in-- in the houses.
And, you know, [INAUDIBLE] I was working near a year-- we was working in the-- in the kitchen.
Then they told us that the Russian-- not-- not a full year, not a full year.
The-- we hear that the Russian, the kitchen is going to Russia.
So we don't want to go to Russia.
So-- so I-- when-- when Hitler was in Russia, when he-- he want to take Russia-- so they took even-- even the-- the cook away from the kitchen.
So I was cook.
I was the cook.
And I got a Russian [? lady ?] on the-- and-- and it was the second [? end. ?] And the Armenian many Jews came.
So I could help.
I-- I took-- I cook a little more soup, a little more [INAUDIBLE].
It wasn't too much, but [INAUDIBLE].
And there, the-- I got a Russian girl from a [INAUDIBLE].
So she took all the food to them, not [INAUDIBLE].
And--
No, no, she was a Russian-- a Russian girl, a goy, no.
But I-- I was [INAUDIBLE] she sleeped with me in-- in one bed.
And we worked together-- a very nice person.
She helped so many [INAUDIBLE] come work in the fields for [INAUDIBLE] partisans.
So I try whatever I could.
I tried to--
The-- the Ukraine?
No Jews over there.
Nobody what-- what-- what [INAUDIBLE].
So I leave Ukraine and took [INAUDIBLE].
In Ukraine, was-- I [? heard ?] in the papers a piece of the first time when I-- I came to my-- to my-- to-- to our town before I left.
So I-- we didn't-- I don't want to go to Russia.
I-- I told [INAUDIBLE], [INAUDIBLE], I am going away.
I don't want to Russia.
I want to go to Israel.
And I-- we must come the American zone.
So and I-- in the night, I left.
She wouldn't-- if she would tell the other people find me.
So [INAUDIBLE] still the war ended.
So I took this as a story also.
Can you hear more a little?
I went out and I-- it was the soldiers going there.
I told them I-- I need-- [INAUDIBLE] was in another camp.
I need those [INAUDIBLE].
I-- I was thinking maybe-- maybe somebody, I left them all in the ghetto.
Maybe somebody's alive.
So I-- they took me.
[INAUDIBLE] They took me, and we was going to [INAUDIBLE].
I came in town.
Can you hear me?
You have time to hear me?
I-- I came-- it is written, this.
I came to [INAUDIBLE].
No-- no one-- no-- [?
Arshun ?] was a synagogue of my father's name.
No, nothing.
I even don't know where-- where I was born, where [INAUDIBLE] was.
But [? our ?] [? rose ?] was near the synagogue.
And in the middle was a cemetery, maybe 100 years old.
We didn't-- in my day, we didn't use this.
Was only for [INAUDIBLE] over there.
And I didn't know where to go.
And I didn't see nobody from my family.
So I sit near-- near the [INAUDIBLE].
And I was so crying that I remember them.
My mother used to say that the [INAUDIBLE] is where [INAUDIBLE] 200 years.
When-- when she got married when she came in this town.
My mother was from another town.
When she got married, she came the deferment in [INAUDIBLE] was over there.
So I was sitting and crying and I didn't know where to go.
Came a Ukraine [? came. ?] She used to come a-- a sister of my oldest sister.
She was sewing only [INAUDIBLE] for ladies, [INAUDIBLE].
For-- no, for men's soles, an apron to wear.
So a dear-- a dear Ukraine, she used to come to my sister.
So she remembers me because I got all the children.
But my mother, she used to come.
The sister was living with Mother.
So she-- so she told me, oh, you Sonia.
I remember you.
So I tell her maybe-- maybe you saw my-- my-- somebody.
She know my mother.
She knows my sister.
I-- we can talk here nothing.
Come with me.
She took my hand.
She helped me to get up.
And she took me.
She told me that yesterday came two men to the-- to the town, to [INAUDIBLE].
From there into Ukraine killed her.
If you were [INAUDIBLE] they will kill you too because they want-- they took everything away.
They don't want [INAUDIBLE].
So she told me [INAUDIBLE] I was going [? with her. ?] So she-- she tell me, I have so much to tell you.
Come with me.
I have to tell you-- to tell you something.
So dear sister, what-- what she was saying-- so when the-- when the year the [INAUDIBLE] tomorrow the ghetto, they'll be every killed.
So she came the night to the-- to [INAUDIBLE] to the Ukraine camp and told me, I will give you up everything I have over there.
There's a grave, and I have everything over there.
I will give you up everything and take me with.
And she got all three children also.
She's the only-- before me, the sister there was.
And she takes me by my hand.
She show me outside by [INAUDIBLE].
It's a little place what is a little [? cave ?] around and around.
All right?
[INAUDIBLE] your sister with the three children.
And now you have everything she brought to me, I want to give you up.
So I told her, no, my dear.
I don't want nothing.
I would like to help my sister or her child.
Things I don't know where to go, and I don't need nothing.
I came in.
I saw in the beds was pillowcases upstairs in the-- of the beds the pillows.
So the pillow cases was written Malke Kulish.
Your name was Malke and Kulish is our name.
It was written.
So--
So she told me she gave me, and she told me to use.
And she will sew [? all those. ?] So I am using it.
But I want to give you up.
You don't have nothing.
And so I didn't took nothing.
She opened-- I-- I have something in my-- a little-- she opened [INAUDIBLE] a-- a coffer.
And-- and she-- she hang up my little [NON-ENGLISH].
[NON-ENGLISH] Yeah, I have this.
Yeah.
And this is what I took.
And-- and later, I start crying for you.
Please, take me to the graves and the forest over there, the graves.
I want to-- to see-- I-- I saw in the grave.
And they made the graves [INAUDIBLE].
Take me to the forest, to the graves.
So she, with her husband, they took me to-- to-- that was our, rightly called, our cemetery.
[INAUDIBLE] the ghetto, all the ghetto, [INAUDIBLE] there.
This is not-- even then I came, it's still [INAUDIBLE].
And I saw where their blood was raining.
Running.
Such a-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] [INAUDIBLE] like [INAUDIBLE].
You know, [NON-ENGLISH SPEECH].
We say a brutal killing.
[NON-ENGLISH SPEECH] And I-- I-- I cried, and I went out.
And I took to go to the forest where I left my children.
I know [INAUDIBLE] they said the last time if I will be alive.
So I-- I wouldn't carry [INAUDIBLE].
When I-- when I came-- when I came-- and so one Ukraine asked me where-- where you going to go?
I told him to the [NON-ENGLISH], to the forest.
What you going there?
So I didn't tell him.
I have to-- I have to over there.
I didn't tell him [INAUDIBLE].
Because they cut down the whole forest.
Not-- not-- no more forest over there.
The-- trees, they cut out all the trees out.
